title: buxus nitidus
intro: Buxus nitidus, also known as boxwood or Japanese box, is a species of flowering plant in the family Buxaceae. It is a small evergreen shrub native to Japan, with glossy dark green leaves and small white or yellowish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which have been reported to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. Buxus nitidus can be prepared as a herbal decoction or tea, and it has also been used in traditional medicine for its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
